info.title: Phonic
info.description: The next-generation voice AI platform to build, observe, and evaluate your voice AI agents.

post.description: Adds a custom phone number to an agent. The user must configure their SIP trunk to point to Phonic's SIP server.

parameters.name: nameOrId
parameters.description: The name or the ID of the agent.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The name of the project containing the agent. Only used when `nameOrId` is a name.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Address
parameters.description: SIP address of the user's SIP trunk. Optional, but if provided, all three SIP headers (X-Sip-Address, X-Sip-Auth-Username, X-Sip-Auth-Password) must be provided. When these headers are provided, call transfers from the agent will use the provided SIP details.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Auth-Username
parameters.description: SIP auth username. Optional, but if provided, all three SIP headers (X-Sip-Address, X-Sip-Auth-Username, X-Sip-Auth-Password) must be provided. When these headers are provided, call transfers from the agent will use the provided SIP details.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Auth-Password
parameters.description: SIP auth password. Optional, but if provided, all three SIP headers (X-Sip-Address, X-Sip-Auth-Username, X-Sip-Auth-Password) must be provided. When these headers are provided, call transfers from the agent will use the provided SIP details.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to add (e.g., "+15551234567").

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the endpoint call.

configuration_endpoint.description: When not `null`, the agent will call this endpoint to get configuration options for calls on this phone number.

400.description: Invalid parameters or phone number already assigned

409.description: The agent is currently being updated. Please try again.

delete.description: Deletes a custom phone number from an agent.

parameters.name: nameOrId
parameters.description: The name or the ID of the agent.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The name of the project containing the agent. Only used when `nameOrId` is a name.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to remove (e.g., "+15551234567").

400.description: Invalid parameters, phone number not assigned to agent, or not a custom phone number

404.description: Agent or phone number not found

409.description: The agent is currently being updated. Please try again.

patch.description: Updates a phone number on an agent.

parameters.name: nameOrId
parameters.description: The name or the ID of the agent.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The name of the project containing the agent. Only used when `nameOrId` is a name.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to add (e.g., "+15551234567").

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the endpoint call.

configuration_endpoint.description: When not `null`, the agent will call this endpoint to get configuration options for calls on this phone number.

400.description: Invalid parameters or phone number already assigned

409.description: The agent is currently being updated. Please try again.

active_conversations.description: Number of conversations currently in progress for this workspace.

max_active_conversations.description: Maximum number of concurrent conversations allowed for this workspace.

logo_url.description: URL of the workspace logo (PNG or SVG), or null if not set.

invite_link_allowed_domains.description: Email domains allowed to join the workspace via invite link.

ip_allowlist.description: IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed to access the workspace.

parameters.description: The ID of the API key to delete.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the API key to rotate.

post.description:
Returns the alternative response(s) the assistant would have
produced for this conversation turn given changes to the agent system prompt.

Only assistant items from ended conversations can be replayed. The
conversation must have an associated agent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the conversation item to replay.

responses.text: Sure — I can help with that. What time works best?

error.message: Cannot replay an item from a live conversation

422.description: Conversation item cannot be replayed (no associated agent, or not an assistant item)

error.message: Cannot replay an item with no associated agent

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The name of the project to list conversations for.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Filter by external ID to get a specific conversation.

parameters.name: duration_min
parameters.description: Minimum duration in seconds.

parameters.name: duration_max
parameters.description: Maximum duration in seconds.

parameters.name: started_at_min
parameters.description: Minimum start date/time. Valid examples: `2025-04-17`, `2025-04-17T02:48:52.708Z`

parameters.name: started_at_max
parameters.description: Maximum start date/time. Valid examples: `2025-04-17`, `2025-04-17T02:48:52.708Z`

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination. Use a conversation ID from `pagination.prev_cursor` to fetch the previous page of conversations. Cannot be used with `after`.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor for forward pagination. Use a conversation ID from `pagination.next_cursor` to fetch the next page of conversations. Cannot be used with `before`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of conversations to return per page.

parameters.name: audio_container
parameters.description: Format of the presigned `audio_url` in each conversation in the response.

200.description: Paginated list (`conversations`, `pagination`) when listing, or a single `conversation` when the `external_id` query parameter matches exactly one conversation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the conversation to get.

parameters.name: audio_container
parameters.description: Format of the presigned `audio_url` in the response.

conversation.welcome_message: Hello {{customer_name}}, this is the {{department}} team. How can I help you today?
conversation.live_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
conversation.post_call_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.system_prompt: You are a helpful {{department}} assistant. The customer's name is {{customer_name}}. Help them book appointments.

parameters.description: The ID of the conversation to cancel.

success.description: Whether the conversation was cancelled successfully.

error.message: Cancellation requested but not acknowledged within the expected time

post.description: Initiates a call to a given phone number using Phonic's Twilio account.

config.welcome_message: Hi {{customer_name}}. How can I help you today?
config.system_prompt: You are an expert in {{subject}}. Be friendly, helpful and concise.

post.description: Initiates a SIP outbound call using user-supplied SIP credentials in headers.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Address
parameters.description: SIP address of the user's SIP trunk. Required.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Auth-Username
parameters.description: SIP auth username, if your provider requires it.

parameters.name: X-Sip-Auth-Password
parameters.description: SIP auth password, if your provider requires it.

from_phone_number.description: Caller ID phone number in E.164 format.

to_phone_number.description: Destination phone number in E.164 format.

dry_run.description: If true, validates the outbound call setup without placing a call. Returns HTTP 200 with `conversation_id` and `twilio_call_sid` set to null.

400.description: Invalid query parameters or body, or missing required headers

error.message: The dialed phone number does not exist.

409.description: The SIP call could not be completed. The `sip_status_code` and `sip_status` fields contain the carrier's SIP response details.

error.message: The SIP call could not be completed.
error.sip_status: Trunk CPS limit exceeded. Region: us1

post.description: Creates a short-lived session token that can be used to authenticate WebSocket connections. Session tokens are useful for client-side applications where you don't want to expose your API key.

session_token.description: The session token to use for authentication.

403.description: Forbidden (only API keys can create session tokens)

error.message: Only API keys can be used to create session tokens

post.description: Creates a short-lived conversation token scoped to a specific agent. Conversation tokens are useful for client-side applications that start a conversation with a single agent without exposing your API key.

conversation_token.description: The conversation token to use for authentication.

403.description: Forbidden (only API keys can create conversation tokens)

error.message: Only API keys can be used to create conversation tokens

post.description: Streams generated speech audio for the provided text.

example.text: Thanks for calling Phonic. How can I help?

200.description: Raw audio bytes streamed as they are generated.

post.description:
Replays an ended conversation by re-running its recorded audio through an
agent. Requires API key or access token authentication. The conversation must
have audio recordings available and an associated agent (or one specified in
the request body).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the conversation to replay.

message.description: Confirmation that the replay has started.

422.description: Conversation cannot be replayed (no audio, deleted recordings, or no agent)

503.description: Insufficient capacity available to start the replay.

sip_status_code.description: The SIP response status code from the carrier (e.g. 403, 486, 603).

sip_status.description: The SIP response reason phrase from the carrier (e.g. "Network Blocked", "Trunk CPS limit exceeded").

LanguageCode.description: ISO 639-1 language codes supported by the agent

text.description: The text to convert to speech.

DataRetentionPolicy.description:
Controls how long transcripts and audio recordings are retained before deletion.
When `zero_data_retention` is `true`, nothing is retained and `transcripts`/`audio_recordings` are omitted.

oneOf.description: Zero data retention mode. No transcripts or audio recordings are retained.

zero_data_retention.description: When `true`, no transcripts or audio recordings are retained.

oneOf.description: Standard data retention with configurable deletion windows.

zero_data_retention.description: Must be `false` for standard data retention.

delete_after_hours.description: Number of hours after which transcripts are deleted. Null means transcripts are retained indefinitely.
Number of hours after which audio recordings are deleted. Null means audio recordings are retained indefinitely.

OutboundNumberPool.description: Pool of phone numbers used for outbound calls.

active.description: E.164 formatted phone numbers available for outbound calls.

blocked.description: E.164 formatted phone numbers that are blocked from being used.

AgentIntegration.description: A third-party integration enabled for an agent.

action_names.description: The integration actions available to the agent.

app_slug.description: The slug identifying the integration app.

BuiltInToolConfig.description: Configuration for a simple built-in tool (`keypad_input` or `natural_conversation_ending`).

speech_before_tool_call.description: Controls whether the agent speaks before the tool is called. `required`: the agent must speak first. `optional`: the model decides. `suppressed`: the agent is strongly instructed to stay silent before the call (best effort).

BuiltInToolConfigs.description: Per-tool configuration overrides for built-in tools, keyed by built-in tool ID (e.g. `tool_natural_conversation_ending`). A built-in tool referenced in `tools` but absent from this map uses its default configuration.

name.description: The name of the agent.

phone_number.description: The phone number that the agent uses to accept calls. `null` if the agent is not associated with a phone number, in which can the agent can be used via WebSockets. This field is deprecated. Use `phone_numbers` instead.

phone_numbers.description: Array of phone numbers that the agent uses to accept phone calls.

project.description: The project the agent belongs to.

timezone.description: The timezone of the agent. Used to format system variables like `{{system_time}}`.

voice_id.description: The voice ID of the agent.

audio_format.description: The audio format of the agent. If the agent has a phone number, the audio format will be `mulaw_8000`.

audio_speed.description: The audio speed of the agent. Must be a multiple of 0.1.

background_noise_level.description: The background noise level of the agent. Must be between 0 and 1.

background_noise.description: The background noise type. Can be "office", "call-center", "coffee-shop", or null.

generate_welcome_message.description: When `true`, the welcome message will be automatically generated and the `welcome_message` field will be ignored.

is_welcome_message_interruptible.description: When `false`, the welcome message will not be interruptible by the user.

websocket_timeout_sec.description: Number of seconds of inactivity before the conversation WebSocket is closed.

welcome_message.description: Message to play when the conversation starts. Ignored when `generate_welcome_message` is `true`.

template_variables.description: Template variables that the agent can use in the welcome message and the system prompt.

tools.description: List of tools available to the agent.

built_in_tool_configs.description: Configuration overrides for built-in tools, keyed by built-in tool ID.

tasks.description: Tasks for the agent to complete during the conversation.

generate_no_input_poke_text.description: Whether to have the no-input poke text be generated by AI.

no_input_poke_sec.description: Number of seconds of silence before sending a poke message. `null` disables the poke message.

no_input_poke_text.description: The message to send after the specified silence. Ignored when generate_no_input_poke_text is true.

no_input_end_conversation_sec.description: Seconds of silence before ending the conversation.

default_language.description: ISO 639-1 language code that sets the agent's default language to recognize and speak. Welcome message and no input poke text should be in this language.

additional_languages.description: Array of additional ISO 639-1 language codes that the agent should be able to recognize and speak. Should not include `default_language`. When `multilingual_mode` is `"auto"`, a maximum of 2 additional languages is allowed.

languages.description: Array of ISO 639-1 language codes that the agent should be able to recognize. This field is deprecated. Use `default_language` and `additional_languages` instead.

multilingual_mode.description: If `"auto"`, each user audio is automatically identified for the language to respond in. If `"request"`, user must request to change language (recommended). If `"initial"` the first turn user audio determines the language for the rest of the conversation.

push_to_talk.description: Push to talk mode. User must send mute/unmute messages to turn on/off listening to audio. Defaults to false.

intelligence_level.description: The intelligence level of the agent. `high` uses a more capable model for more complex reasoning, while `standard` is optimized for lower latency.

boosted_keywords.description: These words, or short phrases, will be more accurately recognized by the agent.

observability_integrations.description: Names of observability integrations enabled for the agent. Each must be one of the supported providers.

pronunciation_dictionary.description: Array of `{ word, pronunciation }` entries. Words must be unique.

min_words_to_interrupt.description: Minimum number of words required to interrupt the assistant.

timeout_ms.description: The timeout for the configuration endpoint in milliseconds.

configuration_endpoint.description: When not `null`, the agent will call this endpoint to get configuration options.

inbound_rollout.description: Float between 0.0 and 1.0 representing the percentage of inbound calls handled by Agent. Requires `phone_number` to be set when less than 1.0.

inbound_rollout_forward_phone_number.description: E.164 formatted phone number where non-agent calls will be forwarded. Required when `inbound_rollout < 1.0`, must be `null` when `inbound_rollout = 1.0`.

vad_prebuffer_duration_ms.description: Voice activity detection prebuffer duration in milliseconds.

vad_min_speech_duration_ms.description: Minimum speech duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

vad_min_silence_duration_ms.description: Minimum silence duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

enable_redaction.description: When `true`, PII and PHI are redacted from text transcripts (e.g. replaced with tags like `[PHONE NUMBER]`) and bleeped from audio recordings after the conversation ends.

slug.description: The URL-friendly slug of the agent.

enable_assistant_backchannel.description: When `true`, the assistant emits backchannel cues (e.g. "mm-hmm") while the user is speaking.

assistant_backchannel_aggressiveness.description: How aggressively the assistant backchannels, from 0 to 1.

integrations.description: Third-party integrations enabled for the agent.

name.description: The name of the agent. Can only contain lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Must be unique within the project.

slug.description: URL-friendly agent slug. Can only contain lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Must be unique within the project.

phone_number.description: When set to `null`, the agent will not be associated with a phone number. When set to `"assign-automatically"`, the agent will be assigned a random phone number. When set to `"custom"`, you must provide `custom_phone_numbers`.

custom_phone_number.description: The custom phone number to use for the agent in E.164 format (e.g., +1234567890). This field is deprecated. Use `custom_phone_numbers` instead.

custom_phone_numbers.description: Array of custom phone numbers in E.164 format (e.g., ["+1234567890", "+0987654321"]). The agent will be able to receive phone calls on any of these numbers. Required when `phone_number` is set to `"custom"`. All phone numbers must be unique.

timezone.description: The timezone of the agent. Used to format system variables like `{{system_time}}`.

audio_format.description: The audio format of the agent.

audio_speed.description: The audio speed of the agent.

background_noise_level.description: The background noise level of the agent.

background_noise.description: The background noise type. Can be "office", "call-center", "coffee-shop", or null.

generate_welcome_message.description: When `true`, the welcome message will be automatically generated and the `welcome_message` field will be ignored.

is_welcome_message_interruptible.description: When `false`, the welcome message will not be interruptible by the user.

websocket_timeout_sec.description: Number of seconds of inactivity before the conversation WebSocket is closed.

welcome_message.description: Message to play when the conversation starts. Can contain template variables like `{{customer_name}}`. Ignored when `generate_welcome_message` is `true`.
welcome_message.example: Hi {{customer_name}}. How can I help you today?

system_prompt.description: Instructions for the conversation. Can contain template variables like `{{subject}}`.
system_prompt.default: Today's date is {{system_date}} {{system_year}}. The day of week is {{system_weekday}}. The current time is {{system_time}}. The timezone for the current conversation is {{system_conversation_timezone}}. Use it only for date/time calculations. Do not infer location from it.
system_prompt.example: You are an expert in {{subject}}. Be friendly, helpful and concise.

template_variables.description: Variables that can be used in the welcome message and the system prompt.

tools.description: Array of built-in or custom tool names to use.

built_in_tool_configs.description: Configuration overrides for built-in tools, keyed by built-in tool ID. Built-in tools not listed here use their default configuration.

tasks.description: Array of task objects with `name` and `description` fields.

generate_no_input_poke_text.description: Whether to have the no-input poke text be generated by AI.

no_input_poke_sec.description: Number of seconds of silence before sending a poke message. `null` disables the poke message.

no_input_poke_text.description: The message to send after the specified silence. Ignored when generate_no_input_poke_text is true.

no_input_end_conversation_sec.description: Seconds of silence before ending the conversation.

enable_assistant_backchannel.description: When `true`, the assistant will produce backchannel responses (e.g. "mm-hmm") while the user is speaking.

assistant_backchannel_aggressiveness.description: How aggressively the assistant produces backchannel responses. Only relevant when `enable_assistant_backchannel` is `true`.

data_retention_policy.description: Controls how long transcripts and audio recordings are retained before deletion.

default_language.description: ISO 639-1 language code that sets the agent's default language to recognize and speak. Welcome message and no input poke text should be in this language.

additional_languages.description: Array of additional ISO 639-1 language codes that the agent should be able to recognize and speak. Should not include `default_language`. When `multilingual_mode` is `"auto"`, a maximum of 2 additional languages is allowed.

languages.description: Array of ISO 639-1 language codes that the agent should be able to recognize. This field is deprecated. Use `default_language` and `additional_languages` instead.

multilingual_mode.description: If `"auto"`, each user audio is automatically identified for the language to respond in. If `"request"`, user must request to change language (recommended). If `"initial"` the first turn user audio determines the language for the rest of the conversation.

push_to_talk.description: Push to talk mode. User must send mute/unmute messages to turn on/off listening to audio. Defaults to false.

intelligence_level.description: The intelligence level of the agent. `high` uses a more capable model for more complex reasoning, while `standard` is optimized for lower latency.

boosted_keywords.description: These words, or short phrases, will be more accurately recognized by the agent.

pronunciation_dictionary.description: Array of `{ word, pronunciation }` entries. Words must be unique.

min_words_to_interrupt.description: Minimum number of words required to interrupt the assistant.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the endpoint call.

configuration_endpoint.description: When not `null`, at the beginning of the conversation the agent will make a POST request to this endpoint to get configuration options.

inbound_rollout.description: Float between 0.0 and 1.0 representing the percentage of inbound calls handled by Agent. Defaults to `1.0`. Requires `phone_number` to be set when less than 1.0.

inbound_rollout_forward_phone_number.description: E.164 formatted phone number where non-agent calls will be forwarded. Required when `inbound_rollout < 1.0`, must be `null` when `inbound_rollout = 1.0`. Defaults to `null`.

vad_prebuffer_duration_ms.description: Voice activity detection prebuffer duration in milliseconds.

vad_min_speech_duration_ms.description: Minimum speech duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

vad_min_silence_duration_ms.description: Minimum silence duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

enable_redaction.description: When `true`, PII and PHI are redacted from text transcripts (e.g. replaced with tags like `[PHONE NUMBER]`) and bleeped from audio recordings after the conversation ends.

mcp_server_ids.description: Array of MCP server IDs to make available to the agent.

observability_integrations.description: Names of observability integrations to enable for the agent. Each must be one of the supported providers.

name.description: The name of the agent. Can only contain lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Must be unique within the project.

slug.description: URL-friendly agent slug. Can only contain lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Must be unique within the project.

phone_number.description: When set to `null`, the agent will not be associated with a phone number anymore. When set to `"assign-automatically"`, the agent will be assigned a random phone number if it doesn't have one yet. If the agent already has a phone number, `"assign-automatically"` has no effect. When set to `"custom"`, you must provide `custom_phone_numbers`.

custom_phone_number.description: The custom phone number to use for the agent in E.164 format (e.g., +1234567890). This field is deprecated. Use `custom_phone_numbers` instead.

custom_phone_numbers.description: Array of custom phone numbers in E.164 format (e.g., ["+1234567890", "+0987654321"]). The agent will be able to receive phone calls on any of these numbers. Required when `phone_number` is set to `"custom"`. All phone numbers must be unique.

timezone.description: The timezone of the agent. Used to format system variables like `{{system_time}}`.

audio_format.description: The audio format of the agent.

audio_speed.description: The audio speed of the agent.

background_noise_level.description: The background noise level of the agent.

background_noise.description: The background noise type. Can be "office", "call-center", "coffee-shop", or null.

generate_welcome_message.description: When `true`, the welcome message will be automatically generated and the `welcome_message` field will be ignored.

is_welcome_message_interruptible.description: When `false`, the welcome message will not be interruptible by the user.

websocket_timeout_sec.description: Number of seconds of inactivity before the conversation WebSocket is closed.

welcome_message.description: Message to play when the conversation starts. Can contain template variables like `{{customer_name}}`. Ignored when `generate_welcome_message` is `true`.
welcome_message.example: Hi {{customer_name}}. How can I help you today?

system_prompt.description: Instructions for the conversation. Can contain template variables like `{{subject}}`.
system_prompt.example: You are an expert in {{subject}}. Be friendly, helpful and concise.

template_variables.description: Variables that can be used in the welcome message and the system prompt.

tools.description: Array of built-in or custom tool names to use.

built_in_tool_configs.description: Configuration overrides for built-in tools, keyed by built-in tool ID. Built-in tools not listed here use their default configuration.

tasks.description: Array of task objects with `name` and `description` fields.

generate_no_input_poke_text.description: Whether to have the no-input poke text be generated by AI.

no_input_poke_sec.description: Number of seconds of silence before sending a poke message. `null` disables the poke message.

no_input_poke_text.description: The message to send after the specified silence. Ignored when generate_no_input_poke_text is true.

no_input_end_conversation_sec.description: Seconds of silence before ending the conversation.

enable_assistant_backchannel.description: When `true`, the assistant will produce backchannel responses (e.g. "mm-hmm") while the user is speaking.

assistant_backchannel_aggressiveness.description: How aggressively the assistant produces backchannel responses. Only relevant when `enable_assistant_backchannel` is `true`.

data_retention_policy.description: Controls how long transcripts and audio recordings are retained before deletion.

outbound_number_pool.description: Pool of phone numbers used for outbound calls. Set to `null` to remove the pool.

procedure_ids.description: Array of procedure IDs associated with the agent.

integrations.description: Array of third-party integrations enabled for the agent.

default_language.description: ISO 639-1 language code that sets the agent's default language to recognize and speak. Welcome message and no input poke text should be in this language.

additional_languages.description: Array of additional ISO 639-1 language codes that the agent should be able to recognize and speak. Should not include `default_language`. When `multilingual_mode` is `"auto"`, a maximum of 2 additional languages is allowed.

multilingual_mode.description: If `"auto"`, each user audio is automatically identified for the language to respond in. If `"request"`, user must request to change language (recommended). If `"initial"` the first turn user audio determines the language for the rest of the conversation.

push_to_talk.description: Push to talk mode. User must send mute/unmute messages to turn on/off listening to audio. Defaults to false.

intelligence_level.description: The intelligence level of the agent. `high` uses a more capable model for more complex reasoning, while `standard` is optimized for lower latency.

boosted_keywords.description: These words, or short phrases, will be more accurately recognized by the agent.

pronunciation_dictionary.description: Array of `{ word, pronunciation }` entries. Words must be unique.

min_words_to_interrupt.description: Minimum number of words required to interrupt the assistant.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the endpoint call.

configuration_endpoint.description: When not `null`, at the beginning of the conversation the agent will make a POST request to this endpoint to get configuration options.

inbound_rollout.description: Float between 0.0 and 1.0 representing the percentage of inbound calls handled by Agent. Requires `phone_number` to be set when less than 1.0.

inbound_rollout_forward_phone_number.description: E.164 formatted phone number where non-agent calls will be forwarded. Required when `inbound_rollout < 1.0`, must be `null` when `inbound_rollout = 1.0`.

vad_prebuffer_duration_ms.description: Voice activity detection prebuffer duration in milliseconds.

vad_min_speech_duration_ms.description: Minimum speech duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

vad_min_silence_duration_ms.description: Minimum silence duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds.

enable_redaction.description: When `true`, PII and PHI are redacted from text transcripts (e.g. replaced with tags like `[PHONE NUMBER]`) and bleeped from audio recordings after the conversation ends.

mcp_server_ids.description: Array of MCP server IDs to make available to the agent.

observability_integrations.description: Names of observability integrations to enable for the agent. Each must be one of the supported providers.

allOf.description:
The behavior differs based on whether the agent already exists:
- For new agents: Default values apply.
            - For existing agents: Only provided fields are updated.

outbound_number_pool.description: Pool of phone numbers used for outbound calls. Set to `null` to remove the pool.

procedure_ids.description: Array of procedure IDs associated with the agent.

name.description: The name of the tool.

description.description: Description of what the tool does.

execution_mode.description: Mode of operation - sync waits for response, async continues without waiting.

parameters.description: Array of parameter definitions for the tool.

endpoint_timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for webhook tools.

tool_call_output_timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for WebSocket tool responses.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to transfer calls to. Set to null if the agent should determine the phone number.

dtmf.description: DTMF digits to send after the transfer connects (e.g., "1234"). Defaults to null. Ignored when dynamic_dtmf is true.

dynamic_dtmf.description: When true, the agent determines the DTMF digits at call time (and may choose to send none); the static dtmf is ignored. Only sent when use_agent_phone_number is true (not on a SIP REFER transfer).

use_agent_phone_number.description: When true, Phonic will transfer the call using the agent's phone number. When false, Phonic will transfer the call using the phone number of the party to whom the agent is connected. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools.

detect_voicemail.description: When true, Phonic will listen in and tell the user if the transfer hits voicemail. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools when use_agent_phone_number is true.

agents_to_transfer_to.description: Array of agent names that the LLM can choose from when transferring. Required for built_in_transfer_to_agent tools.

require_speech_before_tool_call.description: When true, forces the agent to speak before executing the tool.

speech_before_tool_call.description: For built_in_natural_conversation_ending and built_in_keypad_input tools. Whether the agent must speak before calling the tool ("required"), the model decides ("optional"), or the agent must stay silent ("suppressed"). Not used by other tool types.

wait_for_speech_before_tool_call.description: If true, the agent will wait to finish speaking before executing the tool. This is only available for custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

forbid_speech_after_tool_call.description: When true, forbids the agent from speaking after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

allow_tool_chaining.description: When true, allows the agent to chain and execute other tools after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

wait_for_response.description: The agent doesn't typically wait for the response of async custom_websocket tools. When true, makes the agent wait for a response, not call other tools and inform the user of the result. Only available for async custom_websocket tools.

context.description: The static context returned to the agent. Only present for custom_context tools.

item_type.description: Required only when type is "array". The type of items in the array.

name.description: The parameter name.

location.description:
Only applicable for `custom_webhook` tools. Specifies where the parameter should be sent in the webhook request.
- For GET webhooks: defaults to `"query_string"` and `"request_body"` is not allowed.
- For POST webhooks: required, can be either `"request_body"` or `"query_string"`.
- Not allowed for `custom_websocket`, `built_in_transfer_to_phone_number`, or `built_in_transfer_to_agent` tools.
When updating a tool's type or endpoint_method, all parameters must include explicit `location` values.

name.description: The name of the tool. Must be snake_case and unique within the organization.

description.description: A description of what the tool does.

parameters.description:
Array of parameter definitions.
For `custom_webhook` tools with POST method, each parameter must include a `location` field.
For `custom_webhook` tools with GET method, `location` defaults to `"query_string"` if not specified.
For `custom_websocket`, `built_in_transfer_to_phone_number`, and `built_in_transfer_to_agent` tools, `location` must not be specified.

endpoint_method.description: Required for webhook tools. HTTP method for the webhook endpoint.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to transfer calls to. Set to null if the agent should determine the phone number.

dtmf.description: DTMF digits to send after the transfer connects (e.g., "1234"). Defaults to null. Ignored when dynamic_dtmf is true.

dynamic_dtmf.description: When true, the agent determines the DTMF digits at call time (and may choose to send none); the static dtmf is ignored. Only sent when use_agent_phone_number is true (not on a SIP REFER transfer).

use_agent_phone_number.description: When true, Phonic will transfer the call using the agent's phone number. When false, Phonic will transfer the call using the phone number of the party to whom the agent is connected. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools.

detect_voicemail.description: When true, Phonic will listen in and tell the user if the transfer hits voicemail. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools when use_agent_phone_number is true.

agents_to_transfer_to.description: Array of agent names that the LLM can choose from when transferring. Required for built_in_transfer_to_agent tools. All agents must exist in the same project as the tool.

require_speech_before_tool_call.description: When true, forces the agent to speak before executing the tool.

speech_before_tool_call.description: For built_in_natural_conversation_ending and built_in_keypad_input tools. Whether the agent must speak before calling the tool ("required"), the model decides ("optional"), or the agent must stay silent ("suppressed"). Not used by other tool types.

wait_for_speech_before_tool_call.description: If true, the agent will wait to finish speaking before executing the tool. This is only available for custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

forbid_speech_after_tool_call.description: When true, forbids the agent from speaking after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

allow_tool_chaining.description: When true, allows the agent to chain and execute other tools after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

wait_for_response.description: The agent doesn't typically wait for the response of async custom_websocket tools. When true, makes the agent wait for a response, not call other tools and inform the user of the result. Only available for async custom_websocket tools.

context.description: The static context returned to the agent. Required for custom_context tools.

UpdateToolRequest.description:
Fields to update on the tool. All fields are optional; omitted fields are left unchanged.
A tool's `type` cannot be changed after creation.

name.description: The name of the tool. Must be snake_case and unique within the organization.

description.description: A description of what the tool does.

context.description: The static context returned to the agent. Only applicable to custom_context tools.

parameters.description:
Array of parameter definitions.
When updating `endpoint_method`, all parameters must include explicit `location` values.
For `custom_webhook` tools: `location` is required for POST, defaults to `"query_string"` for GET.
For `custom_websocket`, `built_in_transfer_to_phone_number`, and `built_in_transfer_to_agent` tools: `location` must not be specified.

endpoint_method.description: HTTP method for webhook tools. When changing this value, all parameters must include explicit `location` values.

endpoint_headers.description: Headers for webhook tools. Set to null to clear existing headers.

phone_number.description: The E.164 formatted phone number to transfer calls to. Set to null if the agent should determine the phone number.

dtmf.description: DTMF digits to send after the transfer connects (e.g., "1234"). Can be set to null to remove DTMF. Ignored when dynamic_dtmf is true.

dynamic_dtmf.description: When true, the agent determines the DTMF digits at call time (and may choose to send none); the static dtmf is ignored. Only sent when use_agent_phone_number is true (not on a SIP REFER transfer).

use_agent_phone_number.description: When true, Phonic will transfer the call using the agent's phone number. When false, Phonic will transfer the call using the phone number of the party to whom the agent is connected. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools.

detect_voicemail.description: When true, Phonic will listen in and tell the user if the transfer hits voicemail. This is only available for built_in_transfer_to_phone_number tools when use_agent_phone_number is true.

agents_to_transfer_to.description: Array of agent names that the LLM can choose from when transferring. All agents must exist in the same project as the tool.

require_speech_before_tool_call.description: When true, forces the agent to speak before executing the tool.

speech_before_tool_call.description: For built_in_natural_conversation_ending and built_in_keypad_input tools. Whether the agent must speak before calling the tool ("required"), the model decides ("optional"), or the agent must stay silent ("suppressed"). Not used by other tool types.

wait_for_speech_before_tool_call.description: If true, the agent will wait to finish speaking before executing the tool. This is only available for custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

forbid_speech_after_tool_call.description: When true, forbids the agent from speaking after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

allow_tool_chaining.description: When true, allows the agent to chain and execute other tools after executing the tool. Available for custom_context, custom_webhook and custom_websocket tools.

wait_for_response.description: The agent doesn't typically wait for the response of async custom_websocket tools. When true, makes the agent wait for a response, not call other tools and inform the user of the result. Only available for async custom_websocket tools.

name.description: The voice name.

audio_url.description: Presigned URL to the voice sample audio file. Expires in 7 days.

name.description: The name of the project.

id.description: The ID of the project's default agent.

active_conversations.description: Number of conversations currently in progress for this project.

to_phone_number.description: The phone number to call in E.164 format.

dry_run.description: If true, validates the outbound call setup without placing a call. Returns HTTP 200 with `conversation_id` set to null.

background_noise_level.description: Background noise level used in the conversation.

duration_ms.description: Duration of the conversation in milliseconds.

audio_url.description: Presigned URL to the conversation audio file. Expires in 1 day.

ended_by.description: Who or what ended the conversation.

boosted_keywords.description: These words, or short phrases, are more accurately recognized by the model.

pronunciation_dictionary.description: Array of `{ word, pronunciation }` entries. Words must be unique.

min_words_to_interrupt.description: Minimum number of words required to interrupt the assistant.

push_to_talk.description: Push to talk mode. User must send mute/unmute messages to turn on/off listening to audio. Defaults to false.

languages.description: Array of ISO 639-1 language codes recognized by the model. This field is deprecated. Use `default_language` and `additional_languages` instead.

generate_no_input_poke_text.description: Whether the no-input poke text was generated by AI.

no_input_poke_sec.description: Number of seconds of silence before a poke message is sent. `null` means the poke message is disabled.

no_input_end_conversation_sec.description: Seconds of silence before the conversation is ended.

websocket_timeout_sec.description: The WebSocket idle timeout in seconds.

vad_prebuffer_duration_ms.description: Voice activity detection prebuffer duration in milliseconds. `null` when not applicable or unknown (e.g. push-to-talk, or legacy stored conversations).

vad_min_speech_duration_ms.description: Minimum speech duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds. `null` when not applicable or unknown.

vad_min_silence_duration_ms.description: Minimum silence duration for voice activity detection in milliseconds. `null` when not applicable or unknown.

vad_threshold.description: Voice activity detection threshold. `null` when not applicable or unknown.

task_results.description: Results from conversation evaluations and extractions.

call_info.description: Phone call metadata. `null` for non-phone call conversations.

from_phone_number.description: Caller phone number in E.164 format.

to_phone_number.description: Callee phone number in E.164 format.

twilio_call_sid.description: Twilio Call SID. Only present for user SIP trunking calls.

analysis.description: Analysis of the conversation including latencies and interruptions.

is_redacted.description: Whether PII and PHI have been redacted from the conversation.

redacted_transcript.description: The redacted transcript of the conversation. `null` when the conversation is not redacted.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata associated with the conversation.

data_retention_policy.description: Controls how long transcripts and audio recordings are retained before deletion.

deletion_info.description: Information about when transcripts and audio recordings are or were scheduled to be deleted.

transcripts_deleted_at.description: When the transcripts were deleted. `null` if not deleted.

audio_recordings_deleted_at.description: When the audio recordings were deleted. `null` if not deleted.

enable_assistant_backchannel.description: Whether the assistant produced backchannel responses during the conversation.

assistant_backchannel_aggressiveness.description: How aggressively the assistant produced backchannel responses during the conversation.

prev_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the previous page of conversations (newer). Use this value in the `before` query parameter. `null` if there is no previous page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page of conversations (older). Use this value in the `after` query parameter. `null` if there is no next page.

item_idx.description: Index of the item in the conversation.

live_transcript.description: Live transcript of this turn. `null` when the turn has been redacted.

redacted_transcript.description: The redacted transcript of this turn. `null` when the turn is not redacted.

duration_ms.description: Duration of this turn in milliseconds.

system_prompt.description: System prompt used for this assistant turn.

name.description: The tool name.

integration.description: The integration associated with the tool, if any.

endpoint_method.description: HTTP method for webhook tool calls.

endpoint_timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for webhook tool calls.

endpoint_called_at.description: When the webhook endpoint was called (null on error).

query_params.description: Query parameters for webhook tool calls (null on error or when no params).

response_status_code.description: HTTP response status code for webhook tool calls (null on error).

tool_call_output_timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for websocket tool calls.

request_body.description: The request body sent to the tool. Can be any JSON-serializable value.

response_body.description: The response body received from the tool.

timed_out.description: Whether the tool call timed out.

error_message.description: Error message if the tool call failed.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls made by the assistant.

id.description: The ID of the conversation analysis.

interruptions_count.description: Number of interruptions in the conversation.

isDeleted.description: Whether the extraction schema has been deleted.

schema_id.description: ID of the extraction schema to use.

system_prompt.description: The system prompt to use when generating replay responses. Use this to test prompt changes against this conversation turn.

num_responses.description: Number of alternative responses to generate.

agent.description: Name of the agent to replay the conversation with. Defaults to the agent originally associated with the conversation.

responses.description: Alternative responses generated for the conversation item.

text.description: The text the LLM produced for this replay response.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls the LLM would invoke for this replay response - note that the tools will not actually be called during replay.

name.description: The tool name.

endpoint_method.description: HTTP method for webhook tool calls.

query_params.description: Query parameters the LLM produced for webhook tool calls.

context.description: Context returned from custom-context tool calls.

request_body.description: Request body the LLM produced for the tool call.

conversation_id.description: The ID of the conversation that was evaluated.

prompt_id.description: ID of the evaluation prompt to use.

name.description: The evaluation prompt name.
A useful name for referring to this prompt.

prompt.description: Actual evaluation prompt text to evaluate conversations with.

name.description: The name of the task.
A name to identify the API key.
The new name for the API key.
The name of the API key.

api_key.description: The API key secret. Only returned once, when the key is created or rotated.

logo_url.description: URL of the workspace logo. Must be an https URL ending in .png or .svg, or null to clear it.

invite_link_allowed_domains.description: Email domains allowed to join the workspace via invite link.

ip_allowlist.description: IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed to access the workspace.

ttl_seconds.description: Time-to-live for the session token in seconds.

agent_id.description: ID of the agent the conversation token is scoped to.

ttl_seconds.description: Time-to-live for the conversation token in seconds.

conversations.welcome_message: Hello {{customer_name}}, this is the {{department}} team. How can I help you today?
conversations.live_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
conversations.post_call_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.system_prompt: You are a helpful {{department}} assistant. The customer's name is {{customer_name}}. Help them book appointments.

conversation.live_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
conversation.post_call_transcript:
User: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
Assistant: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Hi, I need help with booking an appointment.

items.live_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.post_call_transcript: Of course! I'd be happy to help you book an appointment.
items.system_prompt: You are a helpful {{department}} assistant. The customer's name is {{customer_name}}. Help them book appointments.

bearerAuth.description: Bearer authentication header of the form `Bearer <PHONIC_API_KEY>`. Manage your API keys [here](https://phonic.co/api-keys).
